title: Capture Page Pattern — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: AI Summary: Capture pages for aggregate roots are routed create/edit pages that load foreign-key lookup data in parallel with the aggregate root, stitch the editable aggregate client-side, and submit one Save request through the feature service. The API maps the full aggregate and saves it in one session. Use nxgn-capture-page-title, signals, feature-colocated services, inline errors, and canDeactivate protection.

examples.name: Lifecycle Example 1
examples.code:
Route activates
  │
  ├─ /actions/new    → id() is undefined  → isEditMode() = false → skip load
  └─ /actions/{guid} → id() is valid GUID → isEditMode() = true
                                                    │
                                                effect() fires
                                                    │
                                                loadAction(id)
                                                    │
                                            ┌───────┴───────┐
                                            │               │
                                        fetch DTO     load secondary data
                                            │         (lookups, unique names)
                                            │               │
                                            └───────┬───────┘
                                                    │
                                            populate form signals
                                            snapshot serverIds
                                                    │
                                            ┌───────┴───────┐
                                            │               │
                                        User edits      Child routes
                                        form fields     modify state
                                            │               │
                                            └───────┬───────┘
                                                    │
                                                onSave()
                                                    │
                                            build SaveRequest
                                            (diff children via serverIds)
                                                    │
                                            call service.save()
                                                    │
                                ┌───────────────────┼───────────────────┐
                                │                   │                   │
                            success             success             failure
                           (create)              (edit)                 │
                                │                   │           set errorMessage
                            navigate            reload          toaster error
                           to edit URL           entity
                                │                   │
                        toaster success     toaster success

examples.name: Aggregate root load and save
description: Lookup data and the aggregate root are loaded together, stitched into local state, then saved as one aggregate request.

examples.name: Partial child row save from capture page
description: Child rows are saved one at a time, bypassing aggregate validation and transaction boundaries.

rules.rule_text: Aggregate-root create/edit pages must load foreign-key lookups in parallel with the aggregate root, stitch the editable aggregate client-side, and submit one Save request for the whole aggregate through the feature-colocated service.
Aggregate-root Save requests must include all editable child collections and concurrency state needed for the API to map and save the aggregate in one database session.
Do not save aggregate child rows through separate per-row API calls from the capture page unless the design explicitly models a separate command outside the aggregate-root Save flow.
Do not implement aggregate create/edit in modals; use routed capture pages with canDeactivate protection where unsaved changes are possible.
Aggregate capture pages must consume seeded translation keys for visible labels, validation summaries, lifecycle action text, and prerequisite empty states.

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: These packages **must be installed** before implementing a capture page. If they are missing, install them first. See [Overview — Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages) for setup instructions.

sections.title: Aggregate Root Edit/Create Standard
content: All admin create/edit pages for aggregate roots must follow the Actions/Identity aggregate-root pattern used by Users, Roles, and Translation Keys. The list page navigates to a routed capture page. The capture component loads all foreign-key lookup elements in parallel with the aggregate root when editing. It stitches those lookups and the aggregate DTO into local signal state, including child collections rendered with nxgn-data-grid when needed. Save builds one SaveXRequest for the whole aggregate and calls the feature service save method. The API endpoint maps the full request to entities and commits the aggregate in one database session. Create mode uses EMPTY_GUID or the module's documented create sentinel. Edit mode includes rowVersion or equivalent concurrency state when the aggregate supports concurrency.

sections.title: Lifecycle
content: ├─ /actions/new    → id() is undefined  → isEditMode() = false → skip load
└─ /actions/{guid} → id() is valid GUID → isEditMode() = true

sections.title: Template Structure
content: Key structural elements, using `ActionCaptureComponent` as the canonical example:
1. **Wrapper div** with `[class.d-none]="childActive()"` — hides parent when a child route is active (aggregate pages only)
2. **`nxgn-capture-page-title`** — toolbar with Save/Cancel and optional domain command buttons
3. **Inline saving indicator** — alert with spinner (only while saving)
4. **Inline error message** — alert-danger (only when errorMessage is set)
5. **Loading spinner** — centered spinner while loading entity
6. **Card sections** — Bootstrap cards for form fields, embedded lists, read-only info
7. **`<router-outlet>`** — for aggregate child routes (only when entity has child routes)
<!-- Hide parent content when a child capture route is active -->
<!-- Domain command buttons (edit mode only, non-system entities) -->
<div class="alert alert-info mb-3 d-flex align-items-center gap-2">
<!-- Child route outlet (aggregate capture pages only) -->
Entities without aggregate children (e.g., Workflows without child routes) omit the wrapper div and router outlet:
<!-- Sections with form fields, embedded lists, etc. -->
For classification fields, use `DropdownFieldComponent` with options shaped as `{ id, name }` — matching the [EnumDTO roundtrip pattern](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md):

/** Bound from route param `:id` via withComponentInputBinding() */
// --- Aggregate children (delegated to state for child route access) ---
/** Track server-assigned IDs to distinguish new vs existing items on save */
/** Whether a child route (evidence capture) is active */
/** Unique names loaded from server, excluding current entity in edit mode */
private t(key: string, params?: Record<string, unknown>): string {
// Load entity when route param changes to a valid GUID
// Auto-generate slug from name in create mode only
/** One-way binding + explicit handler to track manual edits */
// 3. Populate aggregate children in state (for child route access)
// 4. Snapshot server IDs for child diffing on save
// 1. Diff children: new items get EMPTY_GUID, existing keep server ID
const msg = (err as { error?: { errors?: { message?: string }[] } })

sections.title: Model Types
content: Model interfaces live in the feature's `.models.ts` file, colocated with the component and service ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md)).

All status, kind, and mode fields MUST use `EnumDTO` (`{ id: number, name: string }`) — the structured type that enables zero-transformation roundtrips between GET and Save. Import `EnumDTO` from `@app/core/models/enum-dto` — never redefine locally. See [Feature Service — Model Rules](feature-service.md#model-rules).
// ✗ Wrong — bare string allows any value, no compile-time safety
// ✗ Wrong — union type requires frontend enum maps and transformation
status: ActionStatus;          // ✗ can't bind to dropdown without transformation
`EnumDTO` fields bind directly to `DropdownFieldComponent` with no transformation. Dropdown options come from enum lookup endpoints ([Feature Service — Lookups](feature-service.md#lookups)):
// Load dropdown options from server — returns EnumDTO[]
// Signal holding the selected option (bound to dropdown)
On load, the DTO's `EnumDTO` field is already the right shape — set it directly:
On save, send the `EnumDTO` back as-is — no extraction needed:
Because status is `EnumDTO`, template comparisons use `.name`:
<!-- ✗ Wrong — bare string comparison (fails with EnumDTO) -->

sections.title: Data Loading
content: Data loading is triggered by an `effect()` in the constructor that watches the route param `id`:

**Why `effect()` over `ngOnInit()`**: The route param is a signal input bound via `withComponentInputBinding()`. An effect reactively fires when it changes, which handles both initial load and subsequent navigation (e.g., browser back/forward). Capstone uses `ngOnInit()` + `history.state`, which breaks on browser refresh because `history.state` is lost.
Every load method follows the same steps:
4. **Populate form signals** — map DTO fields to individual signals
5. **Populate state service** — for child route access (if applicable)
6. **Snapshot server IDs** — `new Set(dto.evidence.map(c => c.id))` for child diffing
7. **Load secondary data** — lookups, unique names, permission types (parallel where possible)
8. **Handle errors** — catch block sets error message via translate
Load lookups, unique values, and other supporting data in parallel using `Promise.all`:
The capture page injects a **feature-colocated service** (see [Feature Service](feature-service.md)). The component never uses `HttpClient` directly.
// ✓ Correct — feature service handles HTTP + firstValueFrom wrapping
// ✗ Wrong — raw HttpClient in a component

sections.title: Duplicate Detection
content: Unique values are loaded from the server to detect duplicate names client-side before save. The server still enforces uniqueness as the final authority — the client check is a UX optimization for immediate feedback.
// Signal holding taken names (loaded during loadSecondaryData)
// In loadSecondaryData (edit mode — excludes current entity):
// In create mode (no entity to exclude):
// Validation computed checks against unique names
See [Validation — Duplicate Detection](validation.md) for the full pattern including aggregate children checking.

sections.title: Saving
content: When the capture page manages an aggregate root with children, the save request must distinguish new items (insert) from existing items (update). The pattern:
1. **On load**: snapshot server-assigned IDs into a `Set<string>`
2. **During editing**: new children get temporary client-side UUIDs via `crypto.randomUUID()`
3. **On save**: map children — if the ID is in the server set, keep it; otherwise replace with `EMPTY_GUID`
// On load — snapshot what the server knows about
// On save — new items get EMPTY_GUID so the API knows to insert
The API's `EntityMapper` uses `EMPTY_GUID` to decide insert vs update, matching the backend [Entity Mapper pattern](../api/entity-mapper.md).
| **Create** | Navigate to `/admin/actions/{newId}` | Show edit mode with server-assigned data (IDs, secrets, timestamps) |
| **Edit** | Reload entity via `loadAction(id)` | Refresh server state (computed fields, child IDs, status changes) |
The catch block extracts the FastEndpoints validation error format:
const msg = (err as { error?: { errors?: { message?: string }[] } })
The API returns `{ errors: [{ message: "..." }] }` for validation failures. The inline error shows the specific validation message; the toaster shows a generic failure notification.

sections.title: Validation
content: Each editable field has a `computed()` that returns a `string[]` of error messages. Empty array = valid.
The `errors` array is passed to form field components: `[errors]="nameErrors()"`. The component renders them below the field.
A single `isFormValid` computed gates the Save button:
- Disabled while saving or loading (prevents double-submit)
- Checks field-level error arrays (catches duplicates, pattern violations)
See [Validation](validation.md) for the full pattern including confirmation dialogs and regex constants.

sections.title: Notifications
content: **Required.** The `ngx-toastr` package must be installed and configured before any capture page is implemented — see [Overview — Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages). Every capture page injects `ToastrService`.
### Two notification channels with distinct purposes:
| **Toaster** (`ToastrService`) | Page-level success/error | After save succeeds, after save fails, after domain command completes | "Action saved", "Failed to save" |
| **Inline alert** (`errorMessage` signal) | Validation errors, API errors | When save returns a validation error, when load fails | "Name is already in use" |
| **Inline spinner** (`saving` signal) | In-process indicator | While save is in flight | "Saving..." |
// ✓ Correct — toaster for transient page-level result
// ✓ Correct — inline for persistent context the user needs while editing
// ✗ Wrong — toaster for validation error (disappears, user loses context)
// ✗ Wrong — inline for success (clutters form, not useful as persistent context)
<!-- Saving spinner: inline because user needs to see context -->
<div class="alert alert-info mb-3 d-flex align-items-center gap-2">
<!-- Error message: inline because it relates to the form -->
**Rule**: Toaster for transient page-level results ("done" / "failed"). Inline for persistent context the user needs while editing (validation, saving indicator, load failure).

sections.title: Translation
content: **Required.** All user-facing strings must use the `translate` pipe in templates or `TranslateService` in component code. No hardcoded English strings in templates or component messages. See [Overview — Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages) for setup.
Every capture page component imports `TranslatePipe` (for templates) and injects `TranslateService` (for code-path messages like toaster and confirmation dialogs).
Keys use **dot-separated hierarchy**: `{feature}.{context}.{key}`. This is the ngx-translate standard and enables namespace-based lazy loading of translation files.
- Always lowercase, dot-separated — never underscores or camelCase in keys
- Feature name matches the route segment (`actions`, `workflows`, `compliance-profiles`)
- Column headers use `{feature}.columns.*` — translated in `ngOnInit` via `firstValueFrom(translate.get(...))`
- Template strings use the `translate` pipe: `'actions.list.title' | translate`

sections.title: Domain Commands
content: Domain commands are actions beyond CRUD that change entity state (deactivate, rotate secret, toggle status, enable/disable action). They follow a consistent pattern:
confirm → execute → reload entity → toaster success
if (!a || a.isCore) return;  // Guard: no action on system entities
await this.loadAction(a.id);  // Reload to reflect new state
Some domain commands don't need confirmation (e.g., enabling an action for an workflow):
- Always reload entity after successful command (server state may have changed)
- Use toaster for success/error (not inline — domain commands are standalone operations)
- Guard against system entities (`isCore`) — disable buttons in template AND check in handler
- Domain command buttons appear in `nxgn-capture-page-title` via `[custom-buttons]` content projection, visible in edit mode only

sections.title: Aggregate Children and Child Routes
content: When the capture page manages an aggregate root with child entities (e.g., Action → Evidences, Consultations):
The parent template includes a `<router-outlet>` that activates child capture routes. When a child route is active, the parent content hides:
A lightweight injectable holds aggregate children in signals, provided at component level so child routes can access them:
2. **Child route activates** → injects state → reads evidence/complianceProfileOptions
3. **Child saves** → calls `state.evidence.update(list => [...list, newItem])` with temp UUID
4. **Child navigates back** → parent is visible again, sees updated children
5. **Parent saves** → diffs children (temp UUID → EMPTY_GUID for new items)

sections.title: Auto-Slug Pattern
content: Create-mode capture pages with a slug field auto-generate the slug from the name. The slug field uses one-way binding + explicit handler to track manual edits:
// Signal tracking whether user has manually edited the slug
// Effect: auto-generate slug from name (create mode only, not manually edited)
// Handler: mark slug as manually edited on first user input
Template uses one-way binding for slug (not `[(value)]`):
<!-- ✗ Wrong — two-way binding triggers handler on every auto-generated change -->
**Why one-way**: Two-way binding would set `slugManuallyEdited` on every auto-generated change. One-way + explicit handler ensures the flag only sets when the **user** types.

sections.title: Common Mistakes
content: // ✗ Wrong — union type requires transformation for dropdown binding

sections.title: Key Rules
content: - Wrap all parent content in `<div [class.d-none]="childActive()">` if entity has child routes
- Use Bootstrap cards (`.card > .card-header + .card-body`) for form sections
- All user-facing strings through `translate` pipe — no hardcoded English
- Inline alerts for validation errors and saving indicator; toaster for success/error
- `DropdownFieldComponent` for classification fields with `{ id, name }` options
- `signal()` for each form field; `computed()` for derived validation and mode detection
- `effect()` in constructor for data loading and auto-slug — never `ngOnInit()`
- `inject()` for all dependencies — no constructor parameters
- Feature service for all HTTP — never raw `HttpClient` ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md))
- `EnumDTO` for all status/kind/mode fields, `NamedDTO` for FK references — never bare `string` or union types ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
- Create mode: navigate to edit URL after save
- Edit mode: reload entity after save
- Always show toaster on success; show toaster + inline error on failure
- Cancel navigates to the list route (`/admin/{entity}`)
- Domain commands reload entity in place

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | Mode detection | `history.state` from grid navigation | Route param GUID regex via `input()` | Survives browser refresh; `history.state` is lost on reload |

| Data loading | `ngOnInit()` → `CapturePageService.loadModel()` | `effect()` watching route param signal | Reactive; handles navigation changes automatically ([ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md)) |

| Service workflow | Centralized `CapturePageService` + `GridPageService` | Feature-colocated services | Vertical slice isolation; features are self-contained ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md)) |

| State management | Mutable model objects + default change detection | Immutable signals + OnPush | Predictable change tracking; explicit state flow ([ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md)) |
| Validation | Template-driven forms (`#form=ngForm` + `form.form.valid`) | Computed signal error arrays | Explicit, testable, OnPush-compatible |
| Post-save navigation | Always navigate to list | Reload entity (edit) / navigate to edit (create) | Aggregate pages need server-assigned state visible (child IDs, secrets, timestamps) |
| Child editing | Modals (`NgbModal`) | Child routes via `<router-outlet>` | Consistent URL model; deep-linkable; per project decision |
| Child data flow | Direct model mutation | Signal update via capture state service | Immutable; trackable; supports OnPush |
| ID generation | `Guid.create().toString()` (third-party library) | `crypto.randomUUID()` (browser native) | No dependency needed; built into modern browsers |
